title: Locust devastation
summary_text: The locust plague strips the land, silences joy, and calls priests and elders to mourn. Joel interprets the plague as a sign that the day of the LORD is near. The chapter summons lament because judgment has entered the covenant land.
key_points: The locust plague strips the land, silences joy, and calls priests and elders to mourn.
Joel interprets the plague as a sign that the day of the LORD is near.
The chapter summons lament because judgment has entered the covenant land.
came to Joel the son of Pethuel.

title: Trumpet and repentance
summary_text: A trumpet calls Zion to fasting and repentance, and the LORD responds with zeal for his land. He promises to restore what the locusts consumed and to pour out his Spirit on all flesh. The chapter turns from grief to hope and from scarcity to abundance.
key_points: A trumpet calls Zion to fasting and repentance, and the LORD responds with zeal for his land.
He promises to restore what the locusts consumed and to pour out his Spirit on all flesh.
The chapter turns from grief to hope and from scarcity to abundance.
inhabitants of the land tremble for the day.

title: Judgment and restoration
summary_text: The LORD gathers the nations into the valley of Jehoshaphat for judgment over how they treated his people. Judah and Jerusalem are restored while the nations are shaken and the LORD dwells in Zion. The book ends with judgment on the oppressors and holiness on God's mountain.
key_points: The LORD gathers the nations into the valley of Jehoshaphat for judgment over how they treated his people.
Judah and Jerusalem are restored while the nations are shaken and the LORD dwells in Zion.
The book ends with judgment on the oppressors and holiness on God's mountain.
